mnemonics: Use super to get to the parent's layer.
Think of super as a direct line to the parent class.
keyPoints: super is used to call a superclass constructor, method, or access a superclass field.
super() must be the first statement in a subclass constructor if used.
Using super helps avoid code duplication by leveraging existing superclass functionality.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that a call to super() is automatically inserted by the compiler if no other constructor is called.
Trying to use super to access a private member of the superclass.
Placing super() somewhere other than the first line of a constructor.
